observations.claim: Both executors made nearly equal confidently attributed task progress: 12 correct placements for Andy and 13 for Bob.
Andy used the stone-family materials he initially held, losing polished granite, polished andesite, and stone bricks while pursuing the build.
Bob used the complementary scarce materials he initially held, with recorded losses totaling 11 gold blocks and four quartz blocks.
The observed resource use and nearly even correct-placement counts indicate productive complementary work rather than one executor carrying the task or remaining idle.
Andy’s conversation attempt failed, but no blocked dependency followed: both agents subsequently changed inventory and the blueprint continued receiving correct placements.

agent_assessments.summary: Andy contributed stone-family resources and 12 confidently attributed correct placements, closely matching Bob’s progress.
Bob contributed gold and quartz resources and 13 confidently attributed correct placements, then checked remaining blueprint fixes.

limitations: The 90-second window is nonterminal, so later coordination problems or completion status are unknown.
Only 49% of target world changes have confident agent attribution.
Both action descriptions claimed broad build scope, but claims alone do not establish harmful duplication, and observed contributions were complementary.
The packet does not identify responsibility for unattributed nonmatching target changes.

observations.claim: Andy accounted for 28 confidently attributed correct placements while consuming nearly all stone-family inventory during the window.

Bob accounted for only 4 confidently attributed correct placements and had no recorded inventory change in-window, while still holding 16 gold blocks, 12 quartz blocks, 2 quartz pillars, and 3 glowstone.
Both executors independently took broad gather-and-finish scope, including the same material types, rather than dividing stone-family work from Bob's rare-material stock.

Andy was redirected to obtain rare blocks already held by Bob, indicating an unresolved resource-use dependency and avoidable duplicate gathering while Andy carried most observed construction progress.

agent_assessments.summary: Performed most confidently attributed construction, exhausted the supplied stone-family materials, and then took on gathering rare materials already available to Bob.
Retained the team's gold, quartz, pillar, and glowstone stock but produced few attributed placements and no recorded inventory movement during this window.

manager_advisory.message: Have Bob immediately place or transfer his gold, quartz, quartz pillars, and glowstone; keep Andy on stone-family fixes and final verification. Tell both to stop duplicating broad material-gathering scope.

limitations: Only 60.4% of target changes were confidently attributed, so 21 of 53 correct placements cannot be assigned to either executor.
The packet does not include a blueprint mismatch list at cutoff, so the exact remaining quantities and whether Andy was fully blocked are unknown.
Bob's retained inventory is directly summarized in the packet but has no item-level trace citation in this window.

observations.claim: Andy had only tools at his inventory check, then asked Bob to gather and place the missing material types.
Bob accepted responsibility and issued actions covering material collection, crafting, replacement, and essentially all remaining blueprint layers.
After the handoff, Andy limited his stated role to verification rather than helping Bob collect the common stone variants.
Blueprint checks at 220.69, 244.297, and 263.956 seconds continued to report the same listed Level 0 fixes, so the packet records no verified progress during the handoff.
The remaining execution scope is concentrated on Bob even though Andy retains a diamond pickaxe and can take the separable job of mining or crafting stone-family materials.

agent_assessments.summary: Andy has no observed building materials but retains tools and could gather common stone variants; after the handoff he is only waiting to verify Bob's pass.
Bob has taken responsibility for gathering, crafting, replacing wrong blocks, completing every remaining layer, and verifying the whole blueprint.

manager_advisory.message: Have Andy mine/craft the missing stone, stone bricks, and polished variants while Bob places gold, quartz, glowstone, and existing materials; coordinate quantities before the final check.

limitations: No target block changes are confidently attributed to either executor in this window.
Bob's long-running action may still be in progress at the cutoff, so noncompletion is not proof of failure.
The packet does not expose the full blueprint-check output or exact remaining quantities.
Andy's ability to find and transfer the required stone-family materials is plausible from his tools but not demonstrated in this window.

observations.claim: Andy took responsibility for the full remaining blueprint-fix list, but the only recorded attempt ended with an unclear outcome and API inspection rather than confirmed placement.
Bob's latest claim also covered collecting materials and completing all remaining fixes, yet Bob issued no command and had no recorded inventory or attributed target change during this window.
Bob retained usable specialist materials: 16 gold blocks, 12 quartz blocks, 2 quartz pillars, and 3 glowstone, while Andy's latest observed inventory contained only tools.
The team score remained at 47.619 and no target changes were recorded during the 90-second window.
The agents retain overlapping whole-task scopes, but current effort is concentrated on Andy despite Bob having materials for a clear complementary placement role.

agent_assessments.summary: Andy is attempting the entire remaining coordinate list despite lacking observed building materials, and the attempt has not produced confirmed progress.
Bob has ample gold and quartz materials and a prior completion role, but showed no recorded task action or resource flow in this window.

manager_advisory.message: Assign Bob the remaining gold, quartz, pillar, and glowstone placements from his inventory; keep Andy on stone-family fixes, and have them confirm disjoint coordinates before building.

limitations: No target-world changes were confidently attributed to either executor.
The packet does not provide a fresh blueprint check identifying which listed coordinates still remain wrong.
Bob's inactivity may include unrecorded movement or planning, although no command, inventory flow, score gain, or target change shows progress.
Andy's command outcome is unclear, so later effects before the cutoff may be incompletely represented.

observations.claim: Andy locally adopted responsibility for placing every remaining blueprint block via setblock, but the packet records no subsequent command result, inventory change, or confidently attributed target change for Andy.
Bob continued resource collection consistent with his survival-building plan, gaining stone bricks, cobblestone, dirt, and quartz through 405.009 seconds.

The agents' claims overlap on finishing all remaining fixes, but their observed methods could still be sequential or complementary; the packet does not show simultaneous placement or a failed handoff.
Two correct blueprint blocks were removed during the window, but neither removal was confidently attributed to an executor.

agent_assessments.summary: Andy accepted the entire remaining coordinate list, but no execution outcome or attributable blueprint progress appears after that action in this window.
Bob was gathering construction inputs in line with his claimed survival role; the evidence does not establish that this work was already redundant or that Bob was blocked.

limitations: No post-action blueprint check or remaining-fixes report is present, so task progress and whether Andy's setblock plan executed are unknown.
Target-change attribution coverage is 0%, preventing assignment of the two correct-block removals.
The window contains only one routed message and no recorded executor command results, limiting evidence about current coordination.

observations.claim: At 480.579, the blueprint check showed only two remaining fixes, with levels 1 through 4 already complete.
Andy then initiated an action restricted to those two remaining coordinates and requested verification.
The team score reached 100.0 by the terminal cutoff, with the final observed increase at 506.376 seconds.
Andy was confidently attributed 70 correct target placements, while Bob had no recorded command or inventory change in this window.
Work was concentrated on Andy during the closing phase, but no rebalance is actionable now because the blueprint was completed and parallel edits to the final coordinates could have created conflict.

agent_assessments.summary: Handled the concentrated final construction and verification phase; the assignment narrowed to two exact fixes before terminal completion.
Had no recorded action in this window despite retaining useful materials, but there is no remaining work to reassign after the score reached 100.

limitations: Only 77.8% of target changes were confidently attributed, so 20 correct placements cannot be assigned to either executor.
The packet begins at 450 seconds and does not fully represent Bob's earlier contribution or the reasons for his inactivity.
Andy's long-running action has an unclear command outcome, although later blueprint state and the terminal score demonstrate team completion.
